extraction.one_line_summary: VAN MOERE Brent won solo from the breakaway with 4+ seconds over the field sprint after Bahrain miscalculated chase effort in middle kilometers
tactical_patterns: Bahrain exhausted themselves chasing mid-distance, couldn't maintain pace to catch lone leader despite dominating final sprint for second.
UAE's McNulty had mechanical inside 35km suggesting domestique work compromised when critical climbing/pacing support disappeared.
GARRISON Ian pulled back from breakaway by Deceuninck to support Jakobsen, revealing team abandoned sprint ambitions when leaders dropped early.
Multiple sprinters (Jakobsen, Pedersen) dropped on Cat-3/4 climbs with 37km remaining, indicating finale profile filters traditional sprint teams.

rider_signals.VAN MOERE Brent: positive: 23yo Belgian dominated all KOM points then soloed to biggest career win, suggesting climbing depth beyond expectations
rider_signals.COBRELLI Sonny: neutral: destroyed field sprint for 2nd but Bahrain's chase pacing strategy failure cost stage win despite having fastest finisher
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: negative: dropped with 37km on Cat-3 finish circuit, couldn't handle even moderate climbing in finale
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: UAE worked for him but dropped on climbs, wasted domestique support from McNulty
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: commentary noted season form inadequate to earn teammate support at Tour, suggesting visible struggles continue
